description_long: This object is a pair of metal pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, commonly used for gripping, bending, or cutting wire and other materials. The handles are long and slightly curved, providing leverage and a comfortable grip for the user. The jaws are broad and flat, with a squared-off tip and a solid, blocky construction, suggesting they are designed for heavy-duty tasks rather than precision work. The pivot point is a single, fixed joint, allowing the two arms to move in opposition and apply force at the jaws. The surface of the pliers appears metallic, with a slightly matte finish and some visible texture, likely from manufacturing or wear. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the exterior surfaces.
description: A sturdy, metallic pair of pliers with long, curved handles and broad, flat jaws, designed for heavy-duty gripping or cutting tasks. The surface is matte and textured, with no visible branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, squared jaws and the curved handles extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curvature of the handles, as well as the thickness of the jaw section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the jaws and the handles' curvature, mirroring the front but with less detail on the jaw faces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the profile of the handles and the solid construction of the jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved metal handles with a smooth, ergonomic contour; no visible insulation or special design elements.
attribute_jaw_design: Broad, flat jaws with squared-off tips; no visible serrations, notches, or wire cutter features; designed for general gripping rather than precision.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot joint; does not appear to allow for adjustable jaw width or compound movement.
attribute_grip_pattern: Handles appear smooth with no visible ribbing, knurling, or rubberized texture.
